chunk_summary: This section outlines a minimum feasible WASH prescription for stunting clinics in high-burden settings, emphasizing safe water, hand hygiene, and child feces/play-space control to reduce diarrhea and inflammation-driven growth suppression.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of WASH interventions in reducing stunting and chronic undernutrition.
Identify three priority WASH behaviors for high-burden settings.
Learn how to link diarrhea management with WASH prevention strategies.
key_facts: WASH counseling is most effective when limited to three prioritized behaviors.
Safe water practices include covered storage, ladle use, and boiling or chlorination when feasible.
Hand hygiene should focus on key moments: after defecation/cleaning child feces and before food preparation/feeding.
Child feces disposal in toilets/latrines and clean play mats reduce enteric exposure.
Every diarrhea episode should trigger early ORS plus zinc supplementation.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Zinc supplementation for acute diarrhea (eLENA) — World Health Organization.
document_key_topics: Minimum feasible WASH prescription for stunting clinics
Hand hygiene and child feces disposal
Linking diarrhea management to WASH interventions
